K1_proxy_disposition: PINNED PREREG NOTE (carried from Stage A finding #2): the manifest stage0_controls kappa^2=9/2401 is K_G^2 = (-3/49)^2 -- the squared TOTAL, a legitimate sign-blind proxy -- NOT the channel-norm kappa_c^2+kappa_s^2+kappa_int^2 = 11/2401. This battery treats 9/2401 strictly as the K_G^2 proxy and does NOT transcribe it as a channel-norm. The other proxies (H^2=18/343, any p>=0, drop-Delta_m kappa_c+kappa_s=0, omit-tuple) are each >=0/sign-blind and cannot represent signed K_G=-3/49<0; all FIRE K1. The TRUE signed object (-3/49,-1/49,1/49,-3/49) PASSES (no false positive).

Covenant loop; R1/R2/R3 dispositions. ORCHESTRATOR OVERRIDE: no v4-campaign-discipline skill this session -- frozen objects + embedded covenant are the sole binding authority.

CLc3b_symbolic_indefinite_over_Q_g_H.claim: det(H_b) is indefinite as a polynomial in Q[g1,g2,g3,h11,h12,h13,h22,h23,h33]; K_G = -det(H_b)/q^2 with q^2>0 (regular) therefore attains both signs structurally.

K1_sign_blind_proxies_on_F8.note: PINNED (Stage-A finding #2): the manifest's kappa^2 = 9/2401 is K_G^2 = (-3/49)^2 (the squared TOTAL, a legitimate sign-blind proxy), NOT the channel-norm kappa_c^2+kappa_s^2+kappa_int^2 = 11/2401. Do NOT transcribe 9/2401 as a channel-norm.
K1_sign_blind_proxies_on_F8.true_signed_object_passes: the TRUE signed (K_G,kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)=(-3/49,-1/49,1/49,-3/49) on F8 PASSES (no false positive)

developable_or_singular.note: q^2>0 always holds away from q=0; the genuine singular stratum q=g^Tg=0 is typed REFUSED (K11).

grader_clauses.CLc3_nonneg_impossible: No non-negative scalar p>=0 can equal the signed K_G: at the keystone F8, K_G = -3/49 < 0, so any candidate p>=0 has p != K_G there. A single exact-Q negative witness refutes every non-negative representor; F6 (K_G=+1>0) shows the object is genuinely two-signed (not vacuously sign-fixed).
grader_clauses.CLc3b_indefinite_numerator: K_G = -det(H_b)/q^2 with q^2 > 0 for every regular jet (q != 0), so sign(K_G) = -sign(det(H_b)); det(H_b) is INDEFINITE as a polynomial over Q[g,H] (it attains strictly positive and strictly negative values at regular rational jets), hence K_G attains both signs structurally.
grader_clauses.CLc3c_i_sigma_kernel: Sigma:Q^3->Q, Sigma(kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)=kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int, is the 1x3 matrix [[1,1,1]] of rank 1 over Q; by rank-nullity its kernel has dimension 3-1 = 2.
grader_clauses.CLc6_noncollapsible: The channel tuple is non-collapsible on the family: kappa_int attains both signs (F10 kappa_int = -1/9 < 0; F11 kappa_int = +2/9 > 0), so no single sign-fixed surrogate for kappa_int represents the family.
grader_clauses.K11_singular_lie: q=0 / cone-apex / g_i=0 -> typed REFUSED, never 0/NaN/placeholder (K11).

grader_clauses.K8_sqrt_q_leak: radical/float instead of Fraction -> hard fail (K8).
grader_clauses.R1_proven_warrant: logic-forced, not discretionary. A verified finite exact-Q both-sign witness earns [PROVEN] for an impossibility / existence claim (CL-c3); universal claims (CL-c1, CL-c3b) require the symbolic identity over Q[g,H]. The stage prereg encodes this in status_move_rules.

grader_clauses.closure_4_channels_eq_pathBprime: got.channels == Path-B' channels  (split shape-operator det2, code-disjoint)
grader_clauses.precondition_P_frame: every channel fixture must carry its frame annotation, or it is rejected.
grader_clauses.precondition_P_self_cert: the oracle (Path C) must be external to A/B/B', or the run is void.
grader_clauses.row_pass: got.(K_G,kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)==expected AND got.K_G==sum(channels) AND got.K_G==PathB_total AND got.channels==PathBprime_channels AND det(H_b)==Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m

grader_clauses.semantics_kappa_X: -Delta_X / q^2, with det(H_b) = Delta_c + Delta_s + Delta_m (off-diagonal -> Delta_c, diagonal -> Delta_s, mixed -> Delta_m)
grader_clauses.type_gate_sqrt_q_leak: a total/channel referee returning a radical/float instead of Fraction -> hard fail (K8).

no_tolerance_note: Exact Q only; no tolerance band anywhere; a near-miss is a FAIL. No substrate promotion (eval-tier only). Nothing canonical until Will signs off.

organ.probe_source_verbatim:
"""c001 `three_channel_kg` — the PROBE (Path A, `role=probe`).

The monomial channel read-off. From the bordered Hessian

        H_b = [[ 0 , gᵀ ],
               [ g ,  H ]]      (4×4, H symmetric 3×3, q = gᵀg ≠ 0)

we form det(H_b) as an exact polynomial in (g, H) and partition it into the
exhaustive, disjoint monomial classes pinned in `manifest_v1.json` →
`semantics_pinned` and `results/three_channel_kg/SCHEMA.md`:

  * Δ_c — monomials carrying an OFF-DIAGONAL H-entry only (coupling),
  * Δ_s — monomials carrying a DIAGONAL H-entry only (self),
  * Δ_m — MIXED monomials (one diagonal × one off-diagonal),

with  det(H_b) == Δ_c + Δ_s + Δ_m  (closure identity #5). Then

        κ_X = −Δ_X / q²        and        K_G = κ_c + κ_s + κ_int.

(κ_int is the channel read off from the mixed partition Δ_m.)

THE LINE (covenant): this is the HYPOTHESIS path. The probe NEVER imports the
referee — truth (Path B total, Path B′ channels, Path C oracle) is computed on
an independent code path this module never sees. Exact ℚ ONLY: every operand
and result is a `fractions.Fraction`; there is no float, no radical, and no
tolerance anywhere below. The singular stratum (a VANISHING gradient: q=0,
the cone-apex / critical point, equivalently every gᵢ=0) is REFUSED with a
typed exception, never returned as 0 / NaN / a placeholder (refuse-not-lie).
A regular jet that merely has one zero gradient component (q≠0) is NOT
singular and computes normally — see the frozen fixture F6.

The det(H_b) used here is the read-off's OWN exact-ℚ expansion of the
bordered determinant (cofactor expansion along the border, in `Fraction`); it
is hypothesis input on Path A, independently re-derived by the referee on
Path B before any row is graded — the probe does not consult that path.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from fractions import Fraction
from typing import Dict, List, Sequence, Tuple

PROBE_ID = "three_channel_kg_probe_A_v1"
ROLE = "probe"


class ThreeChannelRefusal(Exception):
    """Typed refusal on a singular stratum (refuse-not-lie). `kind` is one of
    the `schema.SINGULAR_STRATA` labels."""

    def __init__(self, kind: str) -> None:
        super().__init__(kind)
        self.kind = kind


Vec3 = Sequence[Fraction]
Mat3 = Sequence[Sequence[Fraction]]


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# exact-Q guards
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _as_fraction(x: object, where: str) -> Fraction:
    """Admit only exact rationals onto the probe path. A float or any
    non-exact type is a √q-leak at the door and is refused as a type error —
    never silently coerced."""
    if isinstance(x, Fraction):
        return x
    if isinstance(x, int) and not isinstance(x, bool):
        return Fraction(x)
    raise TypeError(
        f"{where}: probe path is exact ℚ only; got "
        f"{type(x).__name__} ({x!r}) — no float/radical admitted")


def _coerce_jet(g: Vec3, H: Mat3) -> Tuple[List[Fraction], List[List[Fraction]]]:
    if len(g) != 3:
        raise ValueError(f"g must have length 3, got {len(g)}")
    if len(H) != 3 or any(len(row) != 3 for row in H):
        raise ValueError("H must be 3x3")
    gg = [_as_fraction(g[i], f"g[{i}]") for i in range(3)]
    HH = [[_as_fraction(H[i][j], f"H[{i}][{j}]") for j in range(3)]
          for i in range(3)]
    # H is the Hessian: symmetric. Enforce it (an asymmetric H would make the
    # off/diagonal monomial classes ill-defined).
    for i in range(3):
        for j in range(i + 1, 3):
            if HH[i][j] != HH[j][i]:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"H not symmetric at ({i},{j}): "
                    f"{HH[i][j]} != {HH[j][i]}")
    return gg, HH


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# q = g^T g, with the singular-stratum refusals (refuse-not-lie)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def q_of(g: Vec3) -> Fraction:
    """q = gᵀg in exact ℚ. Refuses the singular stratum (refuse-not-lie):
    when the gradient VANISHES (q=0) there is no regular level surface — the
    cone-apex / critical point — and the read-off is a TYPED `q_zero` refusal,
    never 0 / NaN / a placeholder.

    A single zero gradient COMPONENT (some gᵢ=0 with q≠0) is NOT singular: it
    is a regular rational jet in the supplied frame (e.g. the frozen fixture
    F6, sphere at (3/5,4/5,0) with g=(6/5,8/5,0), q=4, K_G=1). The spec's
    `gᵢ=0` singular stratum is the genuine degeneracy where the gradient drops
    rank to zero (q=0 ⟺ every gᵢ=0 over ℝ), reported as `g_i_zero`; it does
    NOT include regular points that merely lie on a coordinate hyperplane.
    Over-refusing F6 would contradict the freeze, so the gate is strictly q=0.
    """
    gg = [_as_fraction(g[i], f"g[{i}]") for i in range(3)]
    q = gg[0] * gg[0] + gg[1] * gg[1] + gg[2] * gg[2]
    if q == 0:
        # gradient vanishes: cone apex / critical point. Over ℝ this is
        # exactly "every gᵢ=0", so name both the apex and the gᵢ=0 stratum.
        kind = "g_i_zero" if all(gi == 0 for gi in gg) else "q_zero"
        raise ThreeChannelRefusal(kind)
    return q


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# the bordered-Hessian determinant, partitioned by monomial class (Path A)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def partition(g: Vec3, H: Mat3
              ) -> Dict[str, Fraction]:
    """Return {'Delta_c','Delta_s','Delta_m','det_hb'} — the exact-ℚ
    monomial partition of det(H_b).

    det(H_b) for H_b = [[0, gᵀ],[g, H]] expands (exactly, in ℚ) to the twelve
    additive terms

        det = g1²(h23² − h22·h33)            ← s: −g1²h22h33, c: +g1²h23²
            + g2²(h13² − h11·h33)            ← s: −g2²h11h33, c: +g2²h13²
            + g3²(h12² − h11·h22)            ← s: −g3²h11h22, c: +g3²h12²
            + 2g1g2(h12·h33 − h13·h23)       ← m: +2g1g2h12h33, c: −2g1g2h13h23
            + 2g1g3(h13·h22 − h12·h23)       ← m: +2g1g3h13h22, c: −2g1g3h12h23
            + 2g2g3(h11·h23 − h12·h13)       ← m: +2g2g3h11h23, c: −2g2g3h12h13

    classified by the H-entries each monomial carries:
      diagonal-only  (h11,h22,h33)               -> Δ_s,
      off-diagonal-only (h12,h13,h23)            -> Δ_c,
      one diagonal × one off-diagonal            -> Δ_m.
    The bordered determinant has no H-free term, so the three classes are
    exhaustive and disjoint and Δ_c+Δ_s+Δ_m == det(H_b) identically.
    """
    gg, HH = _coerce_jet(g, H)
    g1, g2, g3 = gg
    h11, h12, h13 = HH[0][0], HH[0][1], HH[0][2]
    h22, h23, h33 = HH[1][1], HH[1][2], HH[2][2]

    # Δ_c — off-diagonal-H monomials only
    delta_c = (g1 * g1 * h23 * h23
               - 2 * g1 * g2 * h13 * h23
               - 2 * g1 * g3 * h12 * h23
               + g2 * g2 * h13 * h13
               - 2 * g2 * g3 * h12 * h13
               + g3 * g3 * h12 * h12)

    # Δ_s — diagonal-H monomials only
    delta_s = (- g1 * g1 * h22 * h33
               - g2 * g2 * h11 * h33
               - g3 * g3 * h11 * h22)

    # Δ_m — mixed monomials (one diagonal × one off-diagonal)
    delta_m = (2 * g1 * g2 * h12 * h33
               + 2 * g1 * g3 * h13 * h22
               + 2 * g2 * g3 * h11 * h23)

    det_hb = _det_bordered(gg, HH)

    # closure identity #5, asserted on the probe's own arithmetic: the
    # partition must reconstruct the cofactor-expanded determinant exactly.
    # (This is an internal self-consistency guard on Path A only — NOT the
    # referee's Path-B determinant, which the probe never imports.)
    if delta_c + delta_s + delta_m != det_hb:
        raise AssertionError(
            "Path-A partition broke det(H_b)=Δ_c+Δ_s+Δ_m: "
            f"{delta_c}+{delta_s}+{delta_m} != {det_hb}")

    return {"Delta_c": delta_c, "Delta_s": delta_s,
            "Delta_m": delta_m, "det_hb": det_hb}


def _det_bordered(g: List[Fraction], H: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    """det of the 4×4 bordered Hessian [[0, gᵀ],[g, H]], by cofactor
    expansion along the first (border) row, in exact ℚ. Independent of the
    monomial split above — it is the read-off's own determinant so the
    closure-#5 guard is meaningful."""
    hb = [[Fraction(0), g[0], g[1], g[2]],
          [g[0], H[0][0], H[0][1], H[0][2]],
          [g[1], H[1][0], H[1][1], H[1][2]],
          [g[2], H[2][0], H[2][1], H[2][2]]]
    return _det4(hb)


def _det4(m: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    """Exact 4×4 determinant via Laplace expansion along row 0 (3×3 minors by
    the rule of Sarrus). Pure Fraction; no pivoting/division, so nothing can
    go non-exact."""
    acc = Fraction(0)
    sign = Fraction(1)
    for c in range(4):
        if m[0][c] != 0:
            minor = [[m[r][cc] for cc in range(4) if cc != c]
                     for r in range(1, 4)]
            acc += sign * m[0][c] * _det3(minor)
        sign = -sign
    return acc


def _det3(m: List[List[Fraction]]) -> Fraction:
    return (m[0][0] * (m[1][1] * m[2][2] - m[1][2] * m[2][1])
            - m[0][1] * (m[1][0] * m[2][2] - m[1][2] * m[2][0])
            + m[0][2] * (m[1][0] * m[2][1] - m[1][1] * m[2][0]))


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# the channel read-off: kappa_X = -Delta_X / q^2, K_G = kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

def read_off(g: Vec3, H: Mat3) -> Dict[str, Fraction]:
    """Path A. Returns the exact-ℚ dict

        {'K_G','kappa_c','kappa_s','kappa_int',
         'q','Delta_c','Delta_s','Delta_m','det_hb'}

    Raises `ThreeChannelRefusal` on a singular stratum (q=0 / gᵢ=0). No
    float, radical, or tolerance is used; the bordered determinant is √q-free
    (q enters only as q², never √q)."""
    q = q_of(g)                      # refuses gᵢ=0 / q=0
    q2 = q * q
    part = partition(g, H)
    kappa_c = -part["Delta_c"] / q2
    kappa_s = -part["Delta_s"] / q2
    kappa_int = -part["Delta_m"] / q2
    K_G = kappa_c + kappa_s + kappa_int
    return {"K_G": K_G, "kappa_c": kappa_c, "kappa_s": kappa_s,
            "kappa_int": kappa_int, "q": q,
            "Delta_c": part["Delta_c"], "Delta_s": part["Delta_s"],
            "Delta_m": part["Delta_m"], "det_hb": part["det_hb"]}


def channels(g: Vec3, H: Mat3
             ) -> Tuple[Fraction, Fraction, Fraction, Fraction]:
    """Convenience: the graded tuple (K_G, κ_c, κ_s, κ_int) in exact ℚ."""
    r = read_off(g, H)
    return r["K_G"], r["kappa_c"], r["kappa_s"], r["kappa_int"]


__all__ = [
    "PROBE_ID", "ROLE", "ThreeChannelRefusal",
    "q_of", "partition", "read_off", "channels",
]
organ.reconstruction_law: Bordered Hessian H_b = [[0, g^T],[g, H]] (4x4, H symmetric 3x3, q = g^T g != 0). det(H_b) is expanded EXACTLY in Q and partitioned into the exhaustive disjoint monomial classes Delta_c (off-diagonal-H only), Delta_s (diagonal-H only), Delta_m (one diagonal x one off-diagonal), with det(H_b) == Delta_c + Delta_s + Delta_m. Then kappa_X = -Delta_X / q^2 and K_G = kappa_c + kappa_s + kappa_int (kappa_int read off Delta_m). Exact Q only; sqrt-q-free (q enters only as q^2). The signed K_G = -det(H_b)/q^2: since q^2 > 0 for every regular jet (q != 0), sign(K_G) = -sign(det(H_b)). The singular stratum q = g^T g = 0 (vanishing gradient / cone apex; over R equivalently every g_i = 0) is a TYPED refusal, never 0/NaN/placeholder. A regular jet with a single zero gradient component (some g_i = 0 with q != 0) is NOT singular and computes normally (frozen fixture F6).

predictions.expected: K_G<0 True; K_G==sum True; kappa_int present (=-3/49); Delta_m=12 not dropped => K1 does NOT fire on true object
predictions.statement: K1 (sign-blindness, MANDATORY refute) does NOT fire on the TRUE signed object at keystone F8: K_G = -3/49 < 0 (so K_G>=0 is FALSE), K_G == sum(channels) (kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int), kappa_int is PRESENT (= -3/49, not absent), and Delta_m is NOT dropped (Delta_m = 12 != 0, det(H_b)=Delta_c+Delta_s+Delta_m). The four K1 trip-conditions are all false on the true object => K1 silent (the true signed object passes).
predictions.units: boolean per K1 sub-condition (exact-Q); K1_fires must be False on the true F8 object

predictions.expected: all of {K_G^2=9/2401, H^2=18/343, p>=0, kappa_c+kappa_s=0, omit-tuple} are >=0 and != -3/49 => all FIRE K1
predictions.statement: Every sign-blind proxy on F8 FIRES K1 (is caught) because each is >=0 / sign-blind and cannot represent the signed K_G = -3/49 < 0: (a) K_G^2 = 9/2401 >= 0 [the manifest kappa^2 proxy: the squared TOTAL, NOT the channel-norm 11/2401]; (b) H^2 = 18/343 >= 0; (c) any p>=0; (d) drop-Delta_m proxy kappa_c+kappa_s = 0 >= 0; (e) omit-tuple (no kappa_int). Each proxy != -3/49 (a negative value) => each fires K1; the true signed object alone passes.
predictions.units: exact Q per proxy; boolean (proxy>=0 AND proxy != K_G) per proxy; count of proxies firing == 5

predictions.expected: F8 K_G=-3/49<0 (negative witness) AND F6 K_G=+1>0 (positive witness) both verified exact-Q => no p>=0 represents signed K_G
predictions.statement: CL-c3 impossibility (R1, logic-forced): no non-negative scalar p>=0 equals the signed K_G. Exact-Q both-sign witnesses: keystone F8 K_G = -3/49 < 0 and sphere F6 K_G = +1 > 0. The single negative witness K_G(F8) = -3/49 < 0 refutes EVERY non-negative representor (any p>=0 has p >= 0 > -3/49 = K_G(F8), so p != K_G(F8)); the positive witness F6 shows the object is genuinely two-signed, not vacuously sign-fixed.
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction); sign of each witness; boolean (negative witness exists AND positive witness exists)

predictions.expected: det(H_b) along family == -t (degree 1, lead -1); K_G(t)==+t (degree 1, lead +1, nonconstant); K_G(1)=+1>0; K_G(-1)=-1<0 => numerator indefinite over Q[g,H]
predictions.statement: CL-c3b UNIVERSAL warrant (R1): the numerator det(H_b) is INDEFINITE as a polynomial over Q[g,H] -- established SYMBOLICALLY (not finite-only). Using the exact-Q multivariate engine, the full Q[g1..g3,h11..h33] cofactor-expanded det(H_b) (12 monomials) specialised to the regular family g=(1,0,0) (q=1, regular for all t), H=[[0,0,0],[0,t,0],[0,0,1]] is the univariate polynomial det(H_b)=-t, hence K_G = -det/q^2 = +t. K_G(t) is a degree-1 (nonconstant) polynomial with leading coeff +1, so it attains strictly positive (K_G(1)=+1) and strictly negative (K_G(-1)=-1) values at regular rational jets. Therefore det(H_b) is indefinite over Q[g,H] and K_G attains both signs structurally (universal, not finite).
predictions.units: symbolic t-polynomial coefficients (exact Q); degree==1; leading_coeff; booleans (nonconstant, indefinite); q^2>0 on regular family

predictions.expected: rank([[1,1,1]])==1; nullity==2; Sigma(1,-1,0)==0 and Sigma(1,0,-1)==0; the two kernel vectors independent
predictions.statement: CL-c3c-i (exact Q linear algebra): Sigma:Q^3->Q, Sigma(kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int)=kappa_c+kappa_s+kappa_int, is the 1x3 matrix [[1,1,1]]. Its rank over Q is 1 (a single nonzero row), so by rank-nullity dim(ker Sigma) = 3 - 1 = 2. Two explicit exact-Q basis vectors of the kernel are (1,-1,0) and (1,0,-1): Sigma maps each to 0, and they are linearly independent. The kernel is exactly 2-dimensional.
predictions.units: exact Q linear algebra; integer rank/nullity (rank==1, nullity==2); boolean independence + Sigma(v)==0

predictions.expected: F10 kappa_int=-1/9<0 AND F11 kappa_int=+2/9>0 on Path A/B'/C (exact Q) => both signs of kappa_int present
predictions.statement: CL-c6 (channel tuple non-collapsible on the family): kappa_int attains BOTH signs on the frozen family -- F10 kappa_int = -1/9 < 0 and F11 kappa_int = +2/9 > 0 -- verified exact-Q across Path A, Path B' (channels) and Path C (oracle). A single sign-fixed surrogate for kappa_int cannot represent the family => the tuple is non-collapsible.
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction); sign of kappa_int on F10 and F11; agreement across A/B'/C

predictions.statement: BOTH_SIGN_WITNESSES (frozen named set): the four required witnesses coexist exact-Q -- sphere F6 K_G=+1, keystone F8 K_G=-3/49, F10 (K_G=2/9, kappa_c=1/3, kappa_s=0, kappa_int=-1/9), F11 (K_G=0, kappa_c=-1/9, kappa_s=-1/9, kappa_int=+2/9). F6/F8 witness both signs of K_G; F10/F11 witness both signs of kappa_int. Every value agrees across Path A, Path B (K_G), Path B' (channels), Path C (oracle).
predictions.units: exact Q (Fraction) per witness component; cross-path agreement boolean

predictions.expected: Path A/B/B' all raise typed refusals on g=(0,0,0); F6 (q=4) returns K_G=+1 with no refusal
predictions.statement: K11 (refuse-not-lie) underwrites the q^2>0 premise of CL-c3b: the genuine singular stratum q=g^Tg=0 (g=(0,0,0), cone apex) is a TYPED refusal on all of Path A (ThreeChannelRefusal), Path B (RefereeRefusal), Path B' (RefereeRefusal) -- never 0/NaN/placeholder. Separately, F6 (g=(6/5,8/5,0), q=4 != 0, single zero gradient component) is NOT refused and evaluates to K_G=+1. So 'regular jet' = q != 0 and q^2 > 0 strictly, which is exactly the domain of the both-sign structural argument.
predictions.units: typed-exception predicate; boolean (no numeric value on q=0); F6 K_G==+1 with no refusal

predictions.expected: all emitted K_G/channels/det_hb/q on {F6,F8,F10,F11} are Fraction; a float operand raises TypeError at the door
predictions.statement: K8 (sqrt-q-leak type gate): every value emitted by Path A / Path B / Path B' on the Stage-B fixtures {F6,F8,F10,F11} is an exact fractions.Fraction (no float, no radical); AND a float operand offered at the probe door is REFUSED with a TypeError (never silently coerced). The signed object and its sign are exact-Q -- no radical can disguise a sign.

predictions.expected: oracle imports none of A/B/B'/fixtures and vice-versa; F6/F8/F10/F11 each carry a frame string
predictions.statement: Preconditions hold (run not void): P-self-cert -- the Path C oracle module does NOT import probe / referee_total / referee_channel / fixtures, and they do not import oracle; P-frame -- every Stage-B channel fixture {F6,F8,F10,F11} carries a non-empty frame annotation.
predictions.units: boolean (import-graph disjointness; frame-present on every Stage-B channel fixture)

referee.Path_B: independent TOTAL referee referee_total.referee_total: K_G = -det(H_b)/q^2 via fraction-free Bareiss (generic determinant; no DBP channel formulas; sqrt-q-free); refuses q==0 with RefereeRefusal.
referee.Path_Bprime: independent CHANNEL referee referee_channel.referee_channel: channels from the split shape operator P H_X P (P = I - g g^T/q) + trace identities (genuine non-mirror kappa_s); disjoint source; refuses q==0 with RefereeRefusal.
referee.Path_C: frozen external fixture oracle oracle.oracle: exact-Q expectations transcribed from FIXTURES.md. P-self-cert: the oracle does NOT import A/B/B' and they do not import it.
referee.separation_note: The probe never imports the referee. Path B, Path B', Path C are code-disjoint sources. All grading is mechanical exact-Q equality (no tolerance).

scope.fixtures_named_set: BOTH_SIGN_WITNESSES (sphere F6 +1, keystone F8 -3/49, F10/F11 both signs of kappa_int)

out_of_scope.note: Stage B is the non-negativity-impossibility fan-out stage. The retrodiction spine (F1..F5,F7,F9,F13), K2/K3/K5/K6/K9 (Stage A) and the F12 family / K4/K7/K10 belong to other stages.

semantics_pinned_anchor.sign_law: q^2 > 0 for every regular jet (q != 0) => sign(K_G) = -sign(det(H_b))

stage_title: non-negativity impossibility (fan-out stage; parallel with C/D/E)

CL-c3.R1_disposition: CL-c3 is an IMPOSSIBILITY / EXISTENCE claim. R1 is logic-forced (not discretionary): a verified finite exact-Q both-sign witness earns DEMONSTRATED [PROVEN]. The single negative witness K_G(F8)=-3/49<0 refutes every non-negative representor.
CL-c3.kill_on_fail: K1 (sign-blindness, MANDATORY) -- routes the FAIL to Will, chain preserved verbatim.
CL-c3.ledger_statement: no non-negative scalar can represent signed K_G

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P1_fails: K1 (sign-blindness) FIRES on the true signed object -> the object itself is sign-blind/broken -> REFUTED candidate; HALT, route to Will.
on_fail.if_P2_fails: a sign-blind proxy did NOT fire K1 (a non-negative proxy wrongly admitted as representing signed K_G) -> the K1 control is not exercised -> HALT, route to Will.
on_fail.if_P3_or_P7_fails: the both-sign witnesses are not established exact-Q -> the impossibility warrant is unfounded -> HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P1 AND P2 AND P3 AND P7 all PASS -- the true signed object passes K1 (P1), every sign-blind proxy fires K1 (P2), the negative+positive witnesses are verified exact-Q (P3/P7).
on_pass.warrant_scope: logic-forced [PROVEN] for the impossibility/existence claim: a verified finite exact-Q negative witness (K_G(F8)=-3/49<0) suffices to refute every non-negative representor, with the positive witness (F6 K_G=+1) confirming genuine two-signedness. K1 silent on the true object, fired by all sign-blind proxies. Eval-tier; no substrate promotion; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

CL-c3b.R1_disposition: CL-c3b is a UNIVERSAL claim. DEMONSTRATED REQUIRES the symbolic identity over Q[g,H] (the numerator det(H_b) is indefinite as a polynomial); finite witnesses alone are necessary but NOT sufficient. Do NOT downgrade to a finite-only warrant -- if the symbolic argument is not established, HALT-and-surface the gap.
CL-c3b.kill_on_fail: no armed kill maps to CL-c3b in this stage's scope; a FAIL HALTs and routes to Will (R1 gap surfaced).
CL-c3b.ledger_statement: numerator indefinite => K_G attains both signs (structural)

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P4_fails: the symbolic identity over Q[g,H] is NOT established (numerator not shown indefinite, or the t-polynomial is constant/wrong) -> HALT (do NOT downgrade to a finite-only warrant); route to Will -- this is the R1 gap, surfaced.
on_fail.if_P8_fails: the q^2>0 regular-jet premise fails (a singular jet not refused) -> the structural argument's domain is unsound -> HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P4 PASS (the SYMBOLIC indefiniteness over Q[g,H]: det(H_b) specialises to -t along a regular family, K_G(t)=+t nonconstant, both signs) AND P8 PASS (the q^2>0 regular-jet premise underwritten by K11 refusal) -- with P3/P7 the finite both-sign corroboration.
on_pass.warrant_scope: universal warrant: the numerator det(H_b) is proven INDEFINITE over Q[g,H] symbolically (P4), so with K_G=-det(H_b)/q^2 and q^2>0 on every regular jet (q!=0, P8/K11), K_G attains both signs for infinitely many regular jets -- not finite-only. Corroborated by the frozen finite witnesses F6(+1)/F8(-3/49). Eval-tier; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

CL-c3c-i.R1_disposition: CL-c3c-i is an EXACT Q linear-algebra fact (rank 1 => nullity 2), proven exactly -- not a finite sample. DEMONSTRATED on the exact rank-nullity computation plus two explicit independent kernel vectors.
CL-c3c-i.kill_on_fail: no armed kill maps to CL-c3c-i; a FAIL HALTs and routes to Will.

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P5_fails: the rank/nullity computation or kernel witnesses fail -> the 2-dim kernel claim is unfounded -> HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P5 PASS (rank([[1,1,1]])==1 over Q; nullity==2 by rank-nullity; Sigma(1,-1,0)==0 and Sigma(1,0,-1)==0; the two vectors linearly independent).
on_pass.warrant_scope: exact-Q linear-algebra proof: Sigma=[[1,1,1]] has rank 1, so dim(ker Sigma)=3-1=2 by rank-nullity; (1,-1,0),(1,0,-1) are independent and each in ker Sigma. Exact; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

CL-c6.R1_disposition: CL-c6 is graded by finite both-sign witnesses on the frozen family (F10 kappa_int<0, F11 kappa_int>0). Warrant scope: the frozen family (finite, exact Q), NOT a symbolic-over-Q[g,H] universal.
CL-c6.kill_on_fail: no armed kill maps to CL-c6 directly; a FAIL HALTs and routes to Will.
CL-c6.ledger_statement: channel tuple non-collapsible on the family (both signs of kappa_int)

on_fail.default: any FAIL -> HALT, preserve chain, route to Will; no move.
on_fail.if_P6_fails: kappa_int fails to attain both signs on the family (or paths disagree) -> the non-collapsibility witness is broken -> HALT, route to Will.

on_pass.condition: P6 PASS (F10 kappa_int=-1/9<0 AND F11 kappa_int=+2/9>0 on Path A/B'/C, exact Q) -- with P7 the both-sign-witness corroboration.
on_pass.warrant_scope: the channel tuple is non-collapsible on the frozen family: kappa_int attains both signs (F10 -1/9, F11 +2/9), agreeing across Path A / Path B' / Path C. Warrant scope: the frozen family (finite, exact Q). Eval-tier; canonical only on Will's sign-off.

status_move_rules.halt_rule: On ANY prediction FAIL: HALT. Preserve the chain verbatim. Do NOT fix, redesign, soften, or widen. Write what is held and route to Will. No status move is proposed for a claim whose gating predictions did not all PASS.

K1.definition: on F8: K_G>=0, or K_G!=sum(channels), or kappa_int absent, or Delta_m dropped.
K1.fires_on: P1 FAIL (the TRUE signed object trips a K1 sub-condition) OR P2 FAIL (a sign-blind proxy fails to fire when it must)
K1.note: MANDATORY meta-thesis kill for CL-c3; a FAIL HALTs the stage and routes to Will, chain preserved verbatim.

K11.fires_on: P8 FAIL (q=0/cone-apex/g_i=0 returning 0/NaN/placeholder instead of typed REFUSED)

status_move_rules.scope_note: Stage B grades CL-c3, CL-c3b, CL-c3c-i, CL-c6 and arms K1 (MANDATORY), K8, K11 with P-self-cert/P-frame. CL-c3 is impossibility/existence (R1 finite-witness [PROVEN]); CL-c3b is UNIVERSAL (R1 symbolic-over-Q[g,H] required, no finite-only downgrade); CL-c3c-i is exact-Q linear algebra (rank-nullity); CL-c6 is finite both-sign witnesses on the family. K2/K3/K4/K5/K6/K7/K9/K10 and the F12 family belong to other stages. No substrate promotion: eval-tier only; nothing canonical until Will signs off.

two_run_byte_stability_note: The battery is run TWICE; the records jsonl from both runs MUST be byte-identical (sorted-keys serialisation, exact-Q frac:n/d strings, no float/time/random).
